title: Retirement income summary table and Contact the CRA - Canada.ca
content:
## 2025 Income Tax and Benefit Guide for Non-Residents and Deemed Residents of Canada
- Retirement income summary table

## Retirement income summary table
Use the following table to find out where to report your retirement income on your return.
If you were a deemed resident and entered an amount on line 11500 of your return, you are eligible for pension income splitting ( lines 11600 and 21000) and the pension income amount ( line 31400 ).
Use the chart for line 31400 of the Federal Worksheet for Non-Residents and Deemed Residents of Canada to calculate the amount to enter on line 31400 of your return or line 1 of your Form T1032 , Joint Election to Split Pension Income , if applicable.
Boxes 16, 26 (if code 46, 47, 48, 49, 50, or 51 is at box 14 and/or 24)
Boxes 16, 26 (if code 39 is at box 14 and/or 24)
Boxes 16, 26 if (code 07, 14 or 65 is at box 14 and/or 24)
You were 65 or older on December 31, 2025, or You received the amount upon the death of your spouse or common-law partner
- You were 65 or older on December 31, 2025, or
- You received the amount upon the death of your spouse or common-law partner

Use the following chart if you do not have an envelope.
